name: Deploy Containerized Apps to Azure Container Apps
description: Deploy and scale container images on Azure Container Apps with built-in ingress, scaling rules, and revision management.
trendingReason: Container Apps offers a serverless container platform without the complexity of Kubernetes.

steps.title: Install the Container Apps extension
steps.description: Ensures the latest Container Apps CLI extension is installed.

steps.title: Create a Container Apps environment
steps.command: az containerapp env create --name myenv --resource-group myapp-rg --location eastus
steps.description: The environment is a secure boundary around a group of container apps that share networking and logging.

steps.title: Deploy a container app
steps.command: az containerapp create --name myapi --resource-group myapp-rg --environment myenv --image mcr.microsoft.com/k8se/quickstart:latest --target-port 80 --ingress external --min-replicas 1 --max-replicas 5
steps.description: Creates a publicly accessible container app with auto-scaling between 1 and 5 replicas.
steps.tip: Use --ingress internal for apps that should only be reachable within the environment.

steps.title: Set scaling rules
steps.command: az containerapp update --name myapi --resource-group myapp-rg --scale-rule-name http-rule --scale-rule-type http --scale-rule-http-concurrency 50
steps.description: Scales out when concurrent HTTP requests per replica exceed 50.

steps.title: View app logs
steps.command: az containerapp logs show --name myapi --resource-group myapp-rg --follow
steps.description: Streams real-time logs from the running container app.

fullScript:
# Deploy to Azure Container Apps
az extension add --name containerapp --upgrade
az containerapp env create --name myenv --resource-group myapp-rg --location eastus
az containerapp create --name myapi --resource-group myapp-rg --environment myenv --image mcr.microsoft.com/k8se/quickstart:latest --target-port 80 --ingress external --min-replicas 1 --max-replicas 5
az containerapp update --name myapi --resource-group myapp-rg --scale-rule-name http-rule --scale-rule-type http --scale-rule-http-concurrency 50
az containerapp logs show --name myapi --resource-group myapp-rg --follow

faq.question: When should I use Container Apps instead of AKS?
faq.answer: Use Container Apps when you want serverless containers without managing infrastructure. Choose AKS when you need full Kubernetes API access, custom operators, or complex networking.

faq.question: Can I deploy multiple containers in one Container App?
faq.answer: Yes. Container Apps supports sidecar containers. Add additional containers using the --yaml flag with a multi-container configuration.

faq.question: Does Container Apps support private container registries?
faq.answer: Yes. Use 'az containerapp create --registry-server <acr-name>.azurecr.io --registry-username <user> --registry-password <pass>' or configure managed identity for passwordless pulls.
